title: Harness-neutral terminal monitor
goal: Add a robust khazad-doom monitor command that renders an attachable terminal dashboard for either a specific run or the latest active run in a repo.
acceptance: khazad-doom monitor --repo . --latest waits for runs, auto-attaches to the newest active run, keeps showing terminal summaries, and continues waiting for future runs until Ctrl-C.
khazad-doom monitor --run <run-id> follows a specific run and exits successfully only when that run completes; failed/blocked/cancelled/interrupted statuses return an error.
The dashboard is stable and compact, showing run id/status, phase, slice, command, elapsed time, message, recent events, and bounded output tail without cancelling runs on monitor exit.
A non-looping --once mode exists for tests/scripts and renders the same monitor snapshot once.
watch/status --follow remain available as simple text fallback commands.
must_ask_if: The implementation would attempt to auto-open OS windows, tmux panes, or external terminals.
